title: Kondaveedu Fort
tagline: Kondaveedu Fort — heritage city of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Kondaveedu Fort is a historically significant ancient hill fortress located in Kondaveedu, a village in the Chilakaluripet constituency of Palnadu district, Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Kondaveedu Fort is a historically significant ancient hill fortress located in Kondaveedu, a village in the Chilakaluripet constituency of Palnadu district, Andhra Pradesh, India. The site is located 16 miles west of the city of Guntur. Apart from this main fort, there are two other forts (names not known) nearby. Efforts are in progress to classify Kondaveedu Fort as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

topPlaces.name: Bahmani invasion of Orissa
topPlaces.description: The Bahmani invasion of Orissa refers to a series of historical events in the 15th century, marked by conflicts and diplomatic maneuvers between the Gajapati Empire and the Bahmani Sultanate. In 1475, a rebel officer of Bahmani named Bhimraj, revolted at Kondavidu, a Bahmani territory, prompting a complex alliance among the Gajapatis and chiefs of Telangana and Jajnagar.

topPlaces.name: Pedaparimi
topPlaces.description: Pedaparimi is a village in Guntur district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Thullur mandal of Guntur revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Repudi
topPlaces.description: Repudi is a panchayat village in Guntur district of Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Phirangipuram
topPlaces.description: Phirangipuram is a village in Guntur district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Phirangipuram mandal of Guntur revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Guntur
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Guntur, Andhra Pradesh, India was created out of the Diocese of Nellore, 1940. Msgr Thomas Pothacamury was its First Bishop.

topPlaces.name: Vemuluripadu
topPlaces.description: Vemuluripadu is a village in Phirangipuram mandal, Guntur district of Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Pedapalem
topPlaces.description: Pedapalem is a village in Palnadu district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is the headquarters of Atchampet mandal.

topPlaces.name: Nudurupadu
topPlaces.description: Nudurupadu is a village in Guntur district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Phirangipuram mandal of Guntur revenue division.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Bahmani invasion of Orissa & around

In 1475, a rebel officer of Bahmani named Bhimraj, revolted at Kondavidu, a Bahmani territory, prompting a complex alliance among the Gajapatis and chiefs of Telangana and Jajnagar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kondaveedu Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kondaveedu Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kondaveedu Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Vijayawada Airport (64 km) and the nearest railway station is Guntur Junction (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kondaveedu Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kondaveedu Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kondaveedu Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Kondaveedu Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kondaveedu Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bahmani invasion of Orissa, Pedaparimi, Repudi, Phirangipuram, Roman Catholic Diocese of Guntur.
